items.rationale: Canonical primary key in IM model; FK joins from messages.{from,to}_address and tasks.{publisher,assignee}_id depend on it; rename/drop migration out of scope for v0.14.
items.surface_implication: Column survives in schema and internal routing. CLI flags + HTTP body never accept it as input. Surface-level deletion only.

items.rationale: Symmetry with by-id; documented and used by k8s prod callers; mid-cycle rename is gratuitous churn.
items.surface_implication: Path unchanged. Returns 200 with agent record, or 404 for unknown (see Q3).

items.default_recommended: Option A — 404 for unknown; 400 for malformed
items.rationale: Clean separation of bad-data (400) vs missing-resource (404); aligns with REST semantics elsewhere in the API.
items.surface_implication: resolveActor / findAgentByEmail / sendMessage return 404 with { error.code: 'agent_not_found', error.message: "No registered agent for email '<email>'" }.

items.rationale: v0.13.4 fix preserved; downstream WS push and inbox queries integrated against canonical form.
items.surface_implication: Email → canonical mapping server-side in findAgentByEmail. No messages.from_email column added.

items.rationale: Task mandate 'no implicit fallback'; leaving the env reintroduces silent-misrouting bug class this refactor fixes.
items.surface_implication: src/cli/cli.ts drops the if (process.env.BOUNTY_IM_ADDRESS) {...} block; resolveCurrentAgentAddress deleted in src/cli/lib/current-agent.ts. Migration: use `bounty profile use <name>` + EventSource config file.

items.rationale: yargs alias collision would silently merge --server-url -e <url> and --email/-F/-T -e <email> into arrays, breaking the strict-email contract. Standardizing on -u matches the addServerUrlOption helper already shipping for com/{inbox,connect,disconnect}.ts.
items.surface_implication: Task 8 (com/send.ts) changes option from { alias: 'e' } to { alias: 'u' } and routes via addServerUrlOption(). CHANGELOG marks v0.14 BREAKING minor flag-alias rename.

bounty-task/board.ts treated as no-op without publisher-email filter / legacy rejection / test.

com/send.ts --from-email / --to-email flag-only; no active-profile fallback, no parser centralisation, no runtime GREEN body assertion.
